title: Autoscale on queue depth, not just CPU
description: Scale inference replicas on a saturation signal like queue depth or batch latency, which reflects accelerator load better than CPU.
body: Use when configuring autoscaling for a GPU-backed service. Do not scale on CPU utilization — a GPU-bound server can be CPU-idle while requests pile up, so CPU-based scaling never triggers. Actions: scale on queue depth, in-flight batch latency, or accelerator utilization, set targets below saturation, and account for replica warm-up time in the scale-up threshold. Validation signals include replicas added before the queue saturates and latency held under a load ramp. Risk boundary: scaling on the wrong signal leaves the service overloaded while metrics look fine; verify the scaling metric reflects actual accelerator saturation under load.
